# Internal Communications

## Overview

Write effective internal communications using structured formats. This skill covers the most common internal comms types with templates and guidance for each.

## When to Use

- Writing status reports, leadership updates, 3P updates
- Company newsletters
- FAQ responses
- Status reports
- Leadership updates
- Project updates
- Incident reports

## Communication Types

### 3P Updates (Progress, Plans, Problems)

The most common engineering update format:

```markdown
## Progress
- [Completed item 1]
- [Completed item 2]

## Plans
- [Next priority 1]
- [Next priority 2]

## Problems
- [Blocker or risk 1]
- [Blocker or risk 2]
```

**Tips:**
- Be specific — "shipped feature X" not "worked on features"
- Problems should include what you need, not just what's wrong
- Keep it scannable — bullet points, not paragraphs

### Incident Reports

```markdown
# Incident: [Brief Description]

## Summary
[One-paragraph overview of what happened]

## Timeline
- [Time] — [Event]
- [Time] — [Event]

## Impact
- [Who was affected and how]

## Root Cause
[What caused the incident]

## Resolution
[How it was fixed]

## Action Items
- [ ] [Prevention measure 1]
- [ ] [Prevention measure 2]
```

### Project Updates

```markdown
# Project: [Name] — [Date]

## Status
[Green/Yellow/Red] — [One-line summary]

## This Week
- [Accomplishment 1]
- [Accomplishment 2]

## Next Week
- [Plan 1]
- [Plan 2]

## Risks & Blockers
- [Risk 1 with mitigation]
- [Blocker 1 with ask]

## Metrics
- [Key metric 1]: [value]
- [Key metric 2]: [value]
```

### Leadership Updates

```markdown
# [Team] Update — [Period]

## Key Wins
- [Win 1 with business impact]
- [Win 2 with business impact]

## Challenges
- [Challenge 1 with context]
- [Challenge 2 with context]

## Decisions Needed
- [Decision 1 — what we need and by when]

## Metrics Dashboard
- [Metric]: [current] vs [target] ([trend])
```

## General Tips

- Lead with the most important information
- Use the inverted pyramid: conclusion first, details after
- Be specific about what you need from the reader
- Include dates and timelines
- Keep it scannable with headers and bullet points
- Match the tone to the audience (more formal for leadership, more casual for team)
